contents:
image: Deli slice day. Ham, herbed turkey, turkey w Montreal steak spice. Letting em all cool before refrigerating overnight and slicing tomorrow
seitan recipe for @skrimem (for ham specifically - omit tomato paste, dark soy sauce, maple syrup, and paprika for more of a turkey, in which case i’d add poultry spice)
1 block extra firm tofu, drained (i buy 350g packs; use whatever’s in there instead of worrying about weighing it outside of the package) 2Tbsp tapioca starch (or 1T cornstarch) 30g nutritional yeast 1/2Tbsp garlic powder 1/2Tbsp onion powder 2tsp smoked paprika 1tsp liquid smoke 1/2tsp baking powder 2tsp msg (optional) 1tsp dark soy sauce 1tsp tomato paste 1tsp maple syrup 1Tbsp bouillon (i use veg beef bouillon, swap to regular veg or veg chicken for turkey slices) 2Tbsp olive oil couple splashes of water (see instructions) APPROX 180g vital wheat gluten (see instructions) add all ingredients except for the vital wheat gluten water into a large food processor, and process until smooth add water, a tablespoon at a time, until the mixture reaches a hummus-like consistency add the vital wheat gluten and process on high. you may want to remove half the mixture first before adding the vwg if your food processor is on the smaller side. you may need a little more or a little less vwg - horrifically, i eyeball this so my measurement is a guess, but your final “dough” should be firm, uniform, and not dry process until the the consistency goes from small crumbly bits to a more uniform dough turn out onto a clean counter and knead a couple of times to work out any bumpy bits. shape the dough into two logs with your hands place onto a piece of parchment paper large enough to wrap around the entire log. if you’d like, you can add a little more oil here and add a rub - for ham i’d recommend dijon and maple syrup. for turkey i like to add dried sage and/or thyme with black pepper. distribute the rub well, wrap in the paper, and finally wrap tightly with foil. preheat the oven to 350. allow the dough to rest while the oven preheats bake for 1hr, flipping after 30 min 10.allow to cool for at least 30 min without unwrapping. then, unwrap and allow to cool to room temperature before storing in an airtight container the next day, after a night in the fridge, slice or cut into the shapes you want btw - this (for me) makes 2-3 logs of seitan depending on how much you would like of each variety, if you decide to do more than one. even if you want it all the same flavour i’d still recommend breaking it into two equally-sized logs - 1hr in the oven will be enough for 2 or 3 logs, but not enough for 1
title: Deli Slice Seitan

contents:
Measurements will be added next time these are made. Here’s a quick summary for now.
Ingredients Wet okara (cooked okara works well, but raw is fine too since these get cooked a lot — if your okara is dry, add any liquid like plant milk, broth, tomato product, etc.) Vital wheat gluten (VWG) Flour Chick’n bouillon (or any bouillon, or whatever spices you want) Instructions Make the dough Use a food processor to blend for 30 seconds or until well combined. The dough should clump up and come away from the walls of the food processor, like seitan.
Let it autolyse in the food processor for anywhere from 15 minutes to several hours.
After that, you can optionally blend in some dense or frozen tofu, beans, or veggies. Don’t blend too much — these can be chunky.
First bake Squish the dough into nugget shapes and lay them on a baking sheet. Bake for 15 minutes each side at 380°F. After this they’re edible but squishy.
Second cook (pick one) Marinade + air fry — make a marinade and air fry until crispy Pan fry — cut them up and pan fry Panko crust + bake — make a wet dredge with plant milk and flax (optionally with butter or oil) and a dry dredge with panko, starch or flour, and any spices. Wet dredge then dry dredge the nuggets, optionally spray with oil (without oil the panko will stay white and may burn quickly, so keep an eye on them). Bake at 400°F or broil until golden on each side. Notes Loosely based on this recipe by Chez Jorge if you need measurements.
title: Okara Nuggets

contents:
Ingredients 2 cups dry brown lentils 1.7-ish liters water (for boiling) 2 tbsp white vinegar 2.5 tsp tempeh starter (Raprima bulk starter — store in the fridge) Equipment 2 sandwich bags Toothpick (for poking holes in bags) Small kitchen scale Oven with a functioning light (acts as incubator) Large mixing bowl Kitchen towel (for cooling lentils) Strainer Racks Thermometer Instructions Step 1 — Cook the lentils Rinse and cook lentils for 12-13 minutes but NO MORE. Meanwhile poke lots of holes in the sandwich bags w the toothpick.
Step 2 — Cool the lentils Drain and let cool preferably spread out on a kitchen towel laid over a large rack. Come back in 30min or so
Step 3 — Mix in starter Put lentils in a large mixing bowl with vinegar and tempeh starter and mix really well. I put on a mask during this step
Step 4 — Fill the bags Put lentils into the bags using the kitchen scale to ensure you’ve got about the same amount in each (usually about 16oz). After you close the bags, gently even them out and press a bit flat.
Step 5 — Set up your incubator Place on a rack in the oven and turn on the oven light but use other pots to keep your tempeh out of direct oven light because otherwise it will get toooo hot. This will act as your incubator.
Step 6 — Ferment for 2 days Let it grow for 2 days! For the first day leave it alone but after that you will see growth (I took a reference picture, see the moisture and “mold” forming) and this is when you monitor the temp w a thermometer, don’t let it go above 95F for prolonged periods. In the last 18 hours or so, you can take it out of the oven esp if it’s warm at 95F and what I like to do is run a desk fan at low setting and let it air and cool and this I’ve seen to bring it down to 88-92F.
Step 7 — Cook and enjoy Once the tempeh is thick and ready like in the pictures, take it out of the plastic, cut it up, pan sear, splash some soy sauce near the end and thats all you need to enjoy this tasty juicy lentil tempeh.
title: Lentil Tempeh

contents:
Ingredients Cake 1 cup oat flour (for nut free use 1 ¼ cup oat flour and omit the almond flour) ⅓ cup almond flour 1 1/2 tsp baking powder 1 tsp vanilla bean powder (or extract) ½ tsp cinnamon 4-6 pitted Medjool dates 1/3 cup plant milk ⅓ cup apple sauce 1 tbsp apple cider vinegar 2 tbsp cashew butter (Nut Free: Use tahini) Apple Pie Filling 2 apples, diced small ½ tsp cinnamon 1 tsp vanilla bean powder (or extract) ½ cup water 2 tsp lemon juice Date Caramel 6 pitted medjool dates ⅓ cup water or plant milk ½ tsp vanilla bean powder (or extract) Instructions Preheat the oven to 350F.
In a bowl, combine the oat flour, almond flour, baking powder, vanilla bean powder (or add extract to the wet ingredients), and cinnamon. Mix to combine.
In a blender, combine medjool dates, plant milk, apple sauce, apple cider vinegar, and cashew butter. Blend until smooth.
Add the wet mixture to the dry mixture and mix until just combined.
Use a ⅓ cup ice cream scoop or measuring cup to transfer your mixture to a parchment-lined baking sheet. Use a round measuring spoon or coffee scoop to create a dip in the middle for the filling. Wet the spoon to prevent the cake batter from sticking. Optionally wet your fingers and gently smooth the exterior of the cakes.
Bake for 15-17 mins.
While the cakes are baking, prepare your filling by adding the ingredients to a small saucepan. Bring to a gentle simmer, cover, and cook on low until the apples are tender. The time will vary depending on the variety of apple you’re using. When filling is ready, set it aside to cool.
Remove the cakes from the oven and transfer to a cooling rack to cool. Add the apple filling and optionally drizzle with the date caramel. Enjoy!
This batter can also be used for muffins or in ramekins.
Healthy Vegan Eating - Vegan APPLE PIE Mini Cakes!
title: Javant's Apple Cakes

contents:
If you prefer a write up: Brand New Vegan - Jeff Novick Bean Burgers
The Formula
14 oz can no-salt beans half cup rolled oats half cup cooked brown rice (or any starch) 2 tbsp tomato product (tomato sauce, paste, crushed tomatoes etc) 2 tbsp mixed spice (be creative!) mash them all up and bake!
The VHC Theme Song Burgers in Paradise
title: Novick Burger Formula

contents: Ingredients 1.5 cups all-purpose flour 1/2 cup granulated sugar 1 tsp baking powder 1 tsp baking soda 1/4 tsp salt 1/2 cup unsweetened plant milk 1/4 cup lemon juice 1/2 cup olive oil 1 tsp vanilla extract 1 cup fresh strawberries, chopped Instructions Preheat oven to 375°F / 190°C Mix dry ingredients in a large bowl Mix wet ingredients separately then fold into dry Fold in strawberries Fill muffin tin 3/4 full Bake 20-25 minutes until golden
title: Strawberry Love Muffins

title: Strawberry Love Muffins

contents:
Original recipe
I have 3 scoops, 1 large “half cup”, 1 medium “2 tbsp” and 1 small “half tsp”
dry: 1.5 cups ww flour, .5 tsp baking soda, 1.5 tsp baking powder, .25 tsp salt
wet: 2 tbsp flax meal mixed with 6 tbsp water + 1.5 cups soy milk mixed with 2 tbsp apple cider vinegar and 2 tbsp maple syrup or molasses + one mashed banana
Mix dry, mix flax egg, mix milk, mix egg and milk, mash the banana and add some of the milk to it to loosen, add the wet into the flour and stir, add the banana and fold in
cook on 350 f griddle 1/4 cup sized dollops
Notes on “scoops” the 2 tbsp scoop is a coffee scoop, it helps bc the egg is 1 medium scoop flax + 3 medium scoops water, the milk is 3 large scoops + 1 medium scoop vinegar + 1 medium scoop maple syrup
the dry is 3 large scoops of flour, 1 small scoop of baking soda, 3 small scoop of baking powder, dash salt
Converted Recipe 250 g whole wheat flour (350 ml/1.5 cups)
2 g baking soda (2.5 ml/ .5 tsp)
6 g baking powder (7.5 ml / 1.5 tsp)
2 g salt (1.25 ml/ .25 tsp )
350 g soy milk (350 ml/1.5 cups)
40 g molassess (30 ml/2 tbsp)
30 g apple cider vinegar (30ml/2tbsp)
15 g flax meal
85g water (85 ml water/6 tbsp)
Mix dry ingredients
Mix wet ingredients
Mix flax egg to thicken
Mix flax egg with wet ingredients
Mix wet and dry
Cook on griddle at 175c/350 f
title: Hamid's Pancakes

contents:
